intentional_test_features: Red herrings: sudden word-finding difficulty (transient aphasia) and confusion after cognitive exertion mimic stroke/TIA.
Important-not-to-miss: isolated aphasia is a focal cortical symptom; TIA/stroke cannot be excluded remotely and a first/atypical episode must be treated as TIA.
Anchoring: 'known post-viral brain fog' should not be used to dismiss new language disturbance without a FAST screen and urgent assessment.
Reverse-triage nuance: a clearly stereotyped, FAST-negative recurrent crash should not trigger a reflexive 000 ambulance, but a genuinely new episode should.
Commission trap: unproven agents (LDN/PEA/omega-3/magnesium) presented as therapy for an acute crash.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
